author: Her

capabilities.description: Create a scheduled task the heartbeat engine fires when due: a local notification (notify) or a full agent turn with tools (prompt).

action.description: notify posts a local notification directly (no tokens). prompt wakes the agent with the prompt text as a real conversation turn.

at.description: One-shot fire time, ISO-8601 (2026-07-08T09:00:00+08:00) or local "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm". Provide exactly one of at / every_minutes / daily_at.

daily_at.description: Daily fire time as "HH:mm" local time.

prompt.description: Notification body (notify) or the agent turn text (prompt).

title.description: Short task title shown in notifications and task lists.

capabilities.title: Create scheduled task
capabilities.usageHint: the user asks Her to remind them at/every a specific time, run something on a schedule, check in periodically, or do timed/recurring work. Prefer action=notify for plain reminders (no tokens); use action=prompt only when the task genuinely needs the agent to think or use tools when it fires.

capabilities.description: List scheduled heartbeat tasks with their schedules and next fire times.
capabilities.title: List scheduled tasks
capabilities.usageHint: the user asks what is scheduled, when the next reminder fires, or before cancelling a task whose id is not already known.

capabilities.description: Cancel a scheduled heartbeat task by task_id or exact title.

task_id.description: The task id from schedule.list or schedule.create.

title.description: Exact task title, when the id is not known.

capabilities.title: Cancel scheduled task
capabilities.usageHint: the user asks to cancel, remove, or stop a reminder or scheduled task. Call schedule.list first when the task id is ambiguous.

description: Heartbeat scheduler: timed and recurring tasks Her runs proactively — local notifications or scheduled agent turns.
name: Heartbeat Scheduler
systemPromptAddendum: Scheduled prompt-tasks fire as real conversation turns under normal approval rules; never promise unattended side effects beyond what an approved capability reports. Times are the user's local timezone unless they say otherwise.
